name: Ars Therapeutica — structural therapy design pipeline using 12-primitive grammar to diagnose disease deltas and apply TENSOR/MEET operations for therapeutic correction

phase_0.domain_name: Ars Therapeutica — structural therapy design pipeline using 12-primitive grammar to diagnose disease deltas and apply TENSOR/MEET operations for therapeutic correction
phase_0.boundary_condition: The healthy structural type (⊙ criticality, Ħ two-step chirality) that defines the target state for all therapeutic corrections.

VINIT.justification: The uninitialized pathological state before any therapeutic intervention is applied; the void from which therapy begins.

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary of the healthy structural type that contains the entire therapeutic target; the terminal anchor all corrections aim toward.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism that promotes primitives via MAX (e.g., Ħ from 𐑒 to 𐑖) — directed transformation toward the health type.

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent morphism that demotes primitives via MIN (e.g., φ̂ from 𐑣 to ⊙) — conservative floor that pulls super-critical states down.

CLINK.justification: Composition of TENSOR then MEET (or vice versa) into a chained therapeutic sequence; the agglutination of corrective steps.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-recognition of the disease type via delta_primitives and distance computation; the system identifies its own deviation from health.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the disease into its two corrective branches: primitives needing promotion (T-arm) and primitives needing demotion (F-arm).

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the disease type after applying TENSOR and MEET operations, recovering a type that matches the health type on corrected primitives.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: primitives that need promotion (e.g., Ħ, Ω) — the forward, growth-oriented correction path.

EVALF.justification: Negative/failure branch: primitives that need demotion (e.g., φ̂, K) — the conservative, reductive correction path.

ENGAGR.justification: Both TENSOR and MEET held simultaneously without resolution — the paradice of needing two orthogonal operations that cannot be combined into a single compound.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only fixation of the structural diagnosis, delta primitives, and prescribed therapeutic sequence; the immutable prescription.

phase_2.fuse_result: corrected_type (the 12-primitive tuple after applying TENSOR to promotion branch and MEET to demotion branch — must equal the original disease_type with only the targeted primitives changed toward health_type)

phase_3.void_description: untreated disease state — no therapeutic operations applied, all primitives at pathological values
phase_3.true_description: corrected state — all targeted primitives match health_type; therapy successful
phase_3.false_description: uncorrected state — one or more targeted primitives remain at pathological values; therapy failed or incomplete
phase_3.both_description: paradice state — some primitives corrected, others not; the system is simultaneously in a corrected and uncorrected state, held without resolution (e.g., ART-suppressed HIV = MDD)

steps.domain_action: Initialize with the disease_type tuple — the pathological state before any therapy.
Perform structural diagnosis: compute delta_primitives and distance between disease_type and health_type.
Split the disease primitives into two branches: those needing promotion (T-arm) and those needing demotion (F-arm).
On the T-arm, identify primitives requiring TENSOR (MAX) operation — e.g., Ħ from 𐑒 to 𐑖, Ω from 𐑷 to 𐑭.
Apply TENSOR operation to the T-arm: promote each primitive via MAX with a therapeutic system carrying the target value.
On the F-arm, identify primitives requiring MEET (MIN) operation — e.g., φ̂ from 𐑣 to ⊙, K from 𐑺 to 𐑧.
Apply MEET operation to the F-arm: demote each primitive via MIN with a therapeutic system carrying the target value.
Hold both the promoted T-arm and demoted F-arm simultaneously — the dual-component therapy is structurally necessary.
Reconstitute the corrected structural type by fusing the promoted T-arm and demoted F-arm back into a single 12-primitive tuple.
Verify the corrected type: compute new distance to health_type; confirm delta_primitives are resolved.
Permanently record the therapy protocol: disease_type, health_type, delta_primitives, operations applied, and corrected type.
Anchor the system at the health_type boundary — the therapy is complete; the corrected state is the new terminal state.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: ch3mpiler — retrosynthetic molecular design that translates primitive targets (e.g., Ħ=𐑖) into pharmacophore structures and bond disconnections.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: CLINK — the L0→L8 organism-level integration pipeline that orchestrates molecular, cellular, circuit, and clinical layers into a closed-loop feedback system.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: ouroboric pill — a Frobenius-kernel drug delivery system that senses biomarkers and releases therapeutic payloads in response, maintaining state via μ∘δ≈id.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: red-hot_rebis pipeline — sequential phases (e.g., MEET then TENSOR) with temporal ordering determined by primitive dependencies (demotion before promotion for Class B diseases).
phase_5.alfs_store: Imscribing Grammar catalog — the 17,280,000-type crystal of all possible structural types, serving as the canonical reference for disease and health type lookups.

phase_6.cycle_cost: one therapeutic operation (TENSOR or MEET) per primitive corrected
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: disease_type with N primitives differing from health_type
phase_6.post_cycle_state: corrected_type with N' < N primitives differing from health_type (ideally N' = 0)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the structural information is conserved: the disease primitives are not destroyed but transformed into their health counterparts via lattice operations (MAX/MIN), which are deterministic and reversible in the Frobenius sense.
